operator_note: Three things the operator must see. (1) The brief's premise was stale: api/store.py has carried the ladder since 0099739, so the 4 wrong values are reachable through the SHIPPED upload and store paths today whenever OPENAI_API_KEY is set. This iteration measured that exposure, it did not create it - operator decision requested on whether to keep the ladder enabled there while T24 is open. (2) The +20.6pt hold-out headline is stale: re-measured 70.6% -> 79.4% = +8.8pt, because the deterministic path climbed from 55.9% to 70.6% across it-001-it-010. (3) core/label_llm.py promise #2 ('values never go out') is false as written - real person names left the process to the hosted model (T25). Transfer verdict: external +0, confirm +0 correct/+4 wrong, real-carrier scenarios +10 from ONE carrier and TWO captions; the +125 on scenarios is the generator stratum, our own vocabulary read back.

gate_note: G1-G6 ALL PASS (pack 159/159 IoU 0.967727 unchanged, external 23/44, confirm 26/126, all wrong 0; pytest 1626/0 vs baseline 1540; G5 flag-off byte-identical across 77 vs b173e7b; G3 538 fields, 0 regressions; G6 diff subset of allowlist + loop/). G7 FAIL, two causes both named and neither a behaviour finding: (1) gate.py invoked WITHOUT --predict, so predicted=[] and every flip is unpredicted - operator error; (2) 802 of 804 observed flips are fields field_state did not previously enumerate, the concurrent workstream having extended build_field_state.py to cover filled/ and scenarios/ mid-iteration, so registry-newness reads as a flip. The other 2 (confirm::il_dol pay_date, pay_period_end) are provably not this change: il_dol carries no AcroForm and extracts byte-identically with the flag on and off, measured directly. G7 did report target_fields_flipped=[filled::wa_dshs::person_name]. REGISTRY GAP filed a fourth time (it-008, it-010, it-011), now inverted. SEPARATE AND SERIOUS: loop/baseline.json was rebuilt at 04:16 mid-iteration from a tree carrying this iteration's UNCOMMITTED change with REALDOOR_FORM_FIELDS defaulting on; it now records person_name=correct for the filled wa_dshs carrier and all six acroform scenario letters, and its pytest is passed:-1 exit_code:2 (broken collection). An unaccepted change is laundered into the file that defines the accepted state. This commit EXCLUDES loop/baseline.json and loop/tools/; the gate was deliberately NOT re-run against the rebuilt baseline because it would self-certify. Baseline must be rebuilt on a clean master.
corpus_inspection_status: dev (filled and scenarios are dev sets; no hold-out evidence minted)
note: New input source, not a new acceptance rule. Widget internal field NAMES are never consulted - the license is that a widget value is what the page prints, and only the ordinary printed-label rules bind it. Yield 1 field per carrier, not 7: the other six values are read and present in the word stream but the form's printed captions are outside the vocabulary, which is T21 and now reachable.
